title: Cache-Aside Application
triggers: keep hot reads in cache and fall back to the database
application checks cache before querying the database
cache aside read path with database fallback

requiredNodes.label: Application Service
requiredNodes.description: Service that serves requests and manages cache usage.

requiredNodes.label: Primary Datastore
requiredNodes.description: Authoritative record store used on cache misses and writes.

optionalNodes.label: Client
optionalNodes.description: Caller that initiates reads and writes against the application.

optionalNodes.label: Invalidation Worker
optionalNodes.description: Background process that evicts or refreshes stale cache entries.

invariants.rule: The Application Service must retain a fallback path to the Primary Datastore.
invariants.rationale: A cache-aside pattern cannot rely on the cache as the sole source of truth.

invariants.rule: The Cache should not be modeled as the authoritative system of record.
invariants.rationale: The primary datastore owns durable state in a cache-aside design.

invariants.rule: If writes are shown, the cache should also be updated, invalidated, or repopulated.
invariants.rationale: Write-side cache maintenance prevents stale reads.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt is primarily about search indexing, background extraction, or multi-stage pipelines.
antiPatterns.rationale: Those flows are not defined by a cache fallback relationship.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt emphasizes pub-sub or queue-based decoupling between producers and consumers.
antiPatterns.rationale: That structure maps more cleanly to async processing patterns.

clarifications.question: Do you want to show only the read path, or both reads and write-side invalidation?
clarifications.reason: Some cache-aside diagrams focus on reads while others need to make staleness control explicit.

clarifications.question: Should the cache be represented as a shared store or as a private application-side cache?
clarifications.reason: The topology is similar, but labeling differs depending on deployment style.
